summary: Rewrite the gate to read declarations only from the committed lock and walk each kind's `governs` locus, dropping the copy-tree scratch import and the carriage machinery it fed

per.section: The lock and drift — one vocabulary

gate.reason: frame-scale demolition — the SDK front-door gate is discharged (07-05: @dtmd/temper published, layout ratified), so (inplace-lock-producer) no longer blocks, but dropping the scratch import re-touches ~18 test files (all set up via import::run) plus main.rs/import.rs/drift.rs/compose.rs; needs a serialized-chain decomposition ceremony before parallel-safe slices are filed (a blind single filing risks a merge-conflict loop-spin)

edit.description: Drop the copy-tree scratch import in the `Check --harness` branch (main.rs:286-292) and in `session_start_diagnostics` (main.rs:567-571); the gate reads declarations only from the committed lock (20-surface: 'the gate never reads declarations from anywhere but the lock') and walks each kind's `governs` locus for members. Retire `scratch_surface` (main.rs:1076) and the `skill_rule_corpus`/`live_extract_inplace` synthesis where superseded.

Retire the copy-tree pipeline — `import::run`, `write_rollup`, `write_member_surface`, `emit_manifest` — now that no gate path imports into a scratch surface. `init`'s in-place scan survives (re-shaped to SDK-program scaffolding, its own downstream slice).
Retire the `[[member]]` codec (`write_members`/`member_to_table`/`parse_member` and inverses) — the manifest dialect the retired copy-tree produced.
Retire the authored-layer carry-forward / document-carriage path (`carry_representation` and the custom-header twin) — dead once members come from the lock + governs walk, not a re-imported surface.

acceptance: `temper check` on a harness reads its committed SDK-emitted lock (declarations) and walks each kind's governs locus for members, with no scratch copy-tree; a harness with no lock is the unadopted case gated by the embedded default program (fail-loud), never a silent skip.
notes: SDK front-door gate discharged 07-05 (@dtmd/temper published, layout ratified); (inplace-lock-producer) no longer blocks. Remaining blocker is decomposition: the scratch drop touches ~18 test files (grep import::run/scratch/--harness) plus main.rs/import.rs/drift.rs/compose.rs — frame-scale. Parked for a serialized-chain design ceremony; the file list here is the truthful blast radius, not a single-tick scope.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs human release setup — the per-platform engine-binary release workflow, marketplace publish, signing/publish creds — before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end; the SDK scope is now resolved (`@dtmd/temper` published 0.0.1 on 07-05, 0.1.0 reserved for the coordinated release that pins the engine binary), and channel 1's SDK foundation lands via SDK-RECUT-SUBPATH-LAYOUT

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution, 'install is two placements').

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery'). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the SDK authoring face, already published as `@dtmd/temper` — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.1 07-05); ch2 = engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds (marketplace, signing) + the engine-binary release workflow. USPTO name screen still due before launch.
